name: CodeLens
description: The Neural Bridge Between Developer & Codebase. A high-fidelity architectural autopilot synthesizing massive repositories into an interactive neural index.

sections.title: Neural RAG Pipeline
sections.content: Engineered a sophisticated Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline grounded in source code truth. It transcends simple keyword matching by understanding developer intent through high-dimensional vector synthesis and semantic contextualization.

sections.title: Architectural Discovery
sections.content: A glassmorphic, high-focus interface designed to map out structural integrity and logic flow. From onboarding new developers to auditing complex legacy systems, CodeLens provides the strategic depth required for modern engineering.

sections.title: Incremental SHA-256 Indexing
sections.content: Implemented hash-based delta analysis for codebase ingestion. By tracking file changes with SHA-256, only modified fragments are re-embedded, drastically reducing latency and operational costs for private repository analysis.

learnings: Sub-millisecond vector retrieval is the primary bottleneck for building truly conversational architectural interfaces.
AST-aware chunking outperforms standard character-based splitting by preserving the semantic integrity of classes and functions.
Deploying Next.js 16/React 19 applications requires a 'Server-First' mindset for optimal heavy-lifting AI tasks.

name: FerrumDB
description: High-performance, embedded document database engine written from scratch in Rust. Features O(1) performance, AES-256 encryption, and atomic transactions.

sections.title: Bitcask-Inspired AOF
sections.content: Engineered a custom binary log format using an append-only file (AOF) architecture. This design optimizes for high-throughput sequential I/O, ensuring deterministic recovery and crash-resilience through deterministic log replay.

sections.title: Pluggable I/O Abstraction
sections.content: Implemented a sophisticated AsyncFileSystem trait that abstracts disk operations. This allowed for a decorator-based encryption layer, seamlessly wrapping standard I/O with AES-256-GCM authentication tags for each data block.

sections.title: Ferrum Studio
sections.content: Developed a built-in observability dashboard using Axum and lock-free atomic metrics. It provides real-time telemetry on operations per second, lookup counts, and database health metrics at sub-microsecond precision.

learnings: Rust's ownership model is uniquely suited for building lock-free storage engines with high concurrency.
The decorator pattern is powerful for layering cross-cutting concerns like encryption and logging without polluting core logic.
Embedded databases must prioritize memory efficiency; O(1) reads come at the cost of ram-resident indexes.

name: ELYX
description: Local-First Autonomous AI Employee. A multi-platform orchestration engine that monitors email, social media, and ERPs to execute business logic via HITL workflows.

sections.title: Perception Layer
sections.content: Engineered a Python-based watching system using Playwright and standard APIs to monitor disjointed communication channels. The layer synthesizes raw platform events into structured Markdown tasks for the reasoning engine.

sections.title: Obsidian-as-Memory
sections.content: Pioneered a 'Local-First' memory architecture using an Obsidian vault. By treating Markdown files as state-machines, ELYX provides deep transparency—every plan, log, and approval cycle is an immutable technical artifact.

sections.title: HITL Approval Cycles
sections.content: Implemented a rigid Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) protocol for sensitive operations. The orchestrator generates execution plans in a 'Pending' state, requiring the user to physically move files in the vault to authorize high-risk actions.

learnings: Autonomous agents are only as reliable as their 'perception' fidelity; handling platform-specific edge cases is the hardest part.
Markdown is a surprisingly robust state management tool for local-first AI systems due to its human-readable nature.
A truly swappable 'Brain Factory' requires strict output normalization to handle different LLM reasoning styles.

name: Go Load Balancer
description: Production-grade HTTP load balancer with advanced observability. Features multi-strategy routing, distributed tracing, and zero-downtime hot reloads.

sections.title: Advanced Traffic Orchestration
sections.content: Implemented five distinct load-balancing strategies: Round-Robin, Least Connections, Weighted RR, IP Hash, and Random with Two Choices. This allows for granular control over traffic distribution based on backend capacity and persistence requirements.

sections.title: Circuit Breaker & Resilience
sections.content: Engineered a state-aware circuit breaker pattern (Closed, Half-Open, Open) with configurable failure thresholds. By stopping traffic to failing nodes, the system prevents cascading failures and provides deterministic recovery windows.

sections.title: Full-Stack Observability
sections.content: Integrated a comprehensive observability suite featuring Prometheus metrics for P99 latency tracking and OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing. Every request is injected with a unique X-Request-ID for end-to-end auditability.

learnings: Hot-reload configuration using file-watchers is key for minimizing operational friction in high-availability systems.
Passive health checking based on actual proxy error rates detects degradation much faster than periodic active probes alone.
Effective load balancing isn't just about routing; it's about connection pooling and intelligent request retry management.

name: THE SIGNAL
description: Premium AI Intelligence Protocol. A fully automated engine synthesizing global AI news into high-fidelity briefings via a strict 3-3-2-2-1 data protocol.

sections.title: Intelligence Synthesis
sections.content: Engineered a weekly intelligence pipeline that crawls GitHub trending repos and global tech news via NewsAPI. Using Gemini 2.0, the system distills raw data into a premium 3-3-2-2-1 briefing, focusing on high-signal dev tools and AI hardware breakthroughs.

sections.title: Automated Dispatch Workflow
sections.content: Leveraging Inngest's event-driven architecture and Vercel Cron, the engine handles asynchronous task processing with resilient retry logic. It ensures zero-downtime delivery across thousands of global timezones simultaneously.

sections.title: Neural Gamification
sections.content: Implemented a 10-tier loyalty spectrum that evolves the user's interface from 'Alpha Initiate' to 'Signal Architect'. As users reach higher tiers, the entire UI theme dynamically shifts, unlocking total 'Prism Protocol' customization.

learnings: Dynamic timezone delivery requires careful timestamp normalization at the edge to prevent race conditions during global dispatch.
Gamifying a newsletter increases long-term retention by turning consumption into a progression-based technical journey.
Serverless automation via GitHub Actions provides the most cost-effective and reliable orchestrator for consistent weekly content generation.

name: OpsForge
description: Zero-dependency DevOps CLI & REST API toolkit built with Go's standard library. Features 10+ core utilities for load testing, SSL auditing, and network discovery.

sections.title: Zero-Dependency Architecture
sections.content: Engineered a production-ready toolkit using exclusively the Go standard library. By avoiding third-party dependencies, the project achieves an ultra-low attack surface and maximum long-term maintainability for mission-critical DevOps environments.

sections.title: Concurrent Task Orchestration
sections.content: Leverages bounded concurrency models with goroutines and semaphores. The port-scanner and load-tester modules utilize a fixed worker pool to manage thousands of parallel TCP/HTTP connections without exhausting system file descriptors.

sections.title: API-First Extensibility
sections.content: Features a built-in 'devtool serve' mode that exposes all CLI capabilities as a REST API. Includes native Prometheus metrics exposition and a plugin discovery system that automatically registers external binaries from the system PATH.

learnings: Building without external frameworks like Cobra forces a deeper understanding of Go's flag parsing and command-routing primitives.
Structured logging with log/slog is essential for creating machine-readable CLI outputs suitable for modern CI/CD pipelines.
Multi-stage Docker builds using the 'scratch' image provide the most secure and efficient way to distribute compiled binaries.
